differential_diagnoses: Streptococcus anginosus group liver abscess with retroperitoneal extension
Duodenal perforation with retroperitoneal abscess and septic thrombophlebitis
Infective endocarditis (Streptococcus anginosus) with septic emboli
next_steps: Obtain follow-up imaging (repeat CT or MRI of abdomen with IV contrast, preferably with sedation to minimize motion)
Consult infectious disease for extended antibiotic management of Streptococcus anginosus complex
Consult interventional radiology for image-guided drainage of retroperitoneal or liver fluid collections
Monitor for evolution of antiphospholipid syndrome and coordinate anticoagulation strategy with hematology
Perform esophagogastroduodenoscopy to evaluate duodenal pathology
